seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Pydantic AI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Pydantic AI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Pydantic AI agents, hallucinations manifest as structured outputs that pass initial type validation but contain fabricated or inconsistent data, such as invented tool results or nonsensical entity relationships. These issues are particularly insidious because Pydantic's strict typing creates a false sense of reliability, while agent retry logic and nested tool calls can propagate hallucinations deeper into the execution chain. The framework's streaming validation exacerbates this by allowing partially hallucinated responses to commit before full validation fails.

howItManifests.scenario: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected model outputs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns perfectly typed JSON with fabricated customer IDs that don't exist in the database
howItManifests.impact: Downstream systems process ghost records, corrupting analytics and triggering false alerts

howItManifests.scenario: Tool retry logic masking persistent underlying errors
howItManifests.symptom: Database lookup tool hallucinates the same wrong email address across 3 retries before timing out
howItManifests.impact: Customer receives transactional emails at burner addresses, violating compliance

howItManifests.scenario: Dependency injection context not available in nested tool calls
howItManifests.symptom: Nested weather API call hallucinates location data outside the injected user timezone context
howItManifests.impact: Personalized recommendations show irrelevant weather-based suggestions

howItManifests.scenario: Streaming structured responses failing mid-generation validation
howItManifests.symptom: Partial customer profile streams with correct name but hallucinates SSN mid-generation
howItManifests.impact: Frontend displays corrupted PII, violating GDPR and creating compliance nightmares

detection.name: Semantic Consistency Check
detection.description: Validate that structured outputs maintain internal consistency across related fields using Pydantic validators

detection.name: External Fact Verification
detection.description: Cross-reference hallucinated entities against external truth sources before committing

detection.name: Tool Call Provenance Tracking
detection.description: Track tool call lineage to detect hallucinated intermediate results

fixes.name: Fallback Truth Anchors
fixes.description: Implement deterministic fallback values that override hallucinated outputs
fixes.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class SafeCustomerResponse(BaseModel):
    customer_id: str
    email: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: 'unknown@example.com')
    status: str
    confidence: float
    
    @model_validator(mode='after')
    def apply_truth_anchors(self) -> 'SafeCustomerResponse':
        # Hallucination guardrails
        if self.customer_id.startswith('9'):
            # Known hallucination pattern
            self.email = 'suspended@example.com'
            self.confidence = 0.1
            self.status = 'suspended'
        elif self.confidence < 0.7:
            self.status = 'needs_human_review'
        return self

agent = Agent(
    'safe-customer-lookup',
    result_model=SafeCustomerResponse,
    max_retries=1  # Prevent retry loops
)

fixes.name: Nested Context Propagation
fixes.description: Ensure dependency injection flows through nested tool calls

fixes.name: Streaming Validation Windows
fixes.description: Validate streaming chunks incrementally to catch mid-generation hallucinations

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a result_validator in Pydantic AI agents, automatically detecting hallucinations by cross-referencing outputs against ground truth datasets and semantic consistency rules. It captures failure patterns across deployments, building agent-specific hallucination detectors that evolve with your usage patterns. Vex's remediation engine suggests precise field-level fixes and can auto-apply safe fallbacks, reducing MTTR from hours to seconds.

The platform maintains a hallucination risk score per agent, alerting teams before issues cascade to production systems. Vex's dependency injection monitoring catches context-loss hallucinations in nested calls, while its streaming validation handles partial output failures gracefully. Integration takes 3 lines of code and immediately surfaces your most critical hallucination patterns.
vexAutomation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from vex_sdk import Vex

# 3-line Vex integration
vex = Vex(api_key='vex_123')

@pydantic_ai.agent(result_validator=vex.validate_result)
def customer_agent(query: str):
    '''Automatically detects hallucinations in customer data'''
    # Your existing agent logic
    pass

# Vex auto-generates hallucination detectors and fixes
agent = customer_agent
result = agent.run('Get customer profile')

# Vex dashboard shows:
# - Hallucination rate: 12%
# - Top pattern: Fake customer IDs starting with '9'
# - Auto-fix: Replace with 'unknown@example.com'

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and eliminate hallucinations across your Pydantic AI agents in minutes.
